** Scientists successfully decode the [[gorilla]] [[genome]], the last of the [[great ape]] genera to be sequenced.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/science-environment-17239059|title=Gorilla genome could hold key to the human condition|publisher=BBC|author=Pallab Ghosh|date=2012-03-08|access-date=2021-05-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Scally | first1=Aylwyn | last2=Dutheil | first2=Julien Y. | last3=Hillier | first3=LaDeana W. | display-authors=1| title=Insights into hominid evolution from the gorilla genome sequence | journal=Nature | publisher=Springer Science and Business Media LLC | volume=483 | issue=7388 | year=2012 | issn=0028-0836 | doi=10.1038/nature10842 | pages=169–175| pmid=22398555 | pmc=3303130 | bibcode=2012Natur.483..169S |doi-access=free}}</ref>
* 8 March
** A study suggests that donor [[stem cells]] may prevent [[organ rejection]] in imperfectly matched [[organ transplant|transplant]] cases.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.latimes.com/health/la-he-kidney-transplant-stem-cells-20120308,0,6483577.story|title=Study suggests breakthrough in organ transplants |work=Los Angeles Times|author=Melissa Healy|date=2012-03-08|access-date=2021-05-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Leventhal | first1=J. | last2=Abecassis | first2=M. | last3=Miller | first3=J. | display-authors=1 | title=Chimerism and Tolerance Without GVHD or Engraftment Syndrome in HLA-Mismatched Combined Kidney and Hematopoietic Stem Cell Transplantation | journal=Science Translational Medicine | publisher=American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) | volume=4 | issue=124 | date=2012-03-07 | issn=1946-6234 | doi=10.1126/scitranslmed.3003509 | pages=124ra28| pmid=22399264 | pmc=3610325 }}</ref>
** The international [[Daya Bay Reactor Neutrino Experiment|Daya Bay neutrino experiment]] announces the discovery of a new type of [[neutrino oscillation]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.science.org/content/article/physicists-china-nail-key-neutrino-measurement|title=Physicists in China Nail a Key Neutrino Measurement|publisher=Science Magazine|author=Adrian Cho|date=2012-03-08|access-date=2021-05-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=An | first1=F. P. | last2=Bai | first2=J. Z. | last3=Balantekin | first3=A. B. | display-authors=1| title=Observation of Electron-Antineutrino Disappearance at Daya Bay | journal=Physical Review Letters | volume=108 | issue=17 | date=2012-04-23 | issn=0031-9007 | doi=10.1103/physrevlett.108.171803 | article-number=171803| pmid=22680853 | arxiv=1203.1669 | bibcode=2012PhRvL.108q1803A | s2cid=16580300 }}</ref>
* 9 March – US researchers announce a breakthrough in treating [[AIDS]], using a cancer drug to attack HIV inside certain immune-system cells, which were previously difficult to reach with treatments.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bloomberg.com/news/2012-03-08/aids-cure-quest-advances-as-merck-cancer-medicine-attacks-hidden-hiv.html|title=AIDS Cure Quest Advances as Cancer Drug Finds Hidden HIV|publisher=Bloomberg News|author=Robert Langreth and Shannon Pettypiece|date=2012-07-25|access-date=2021-05-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160919184912/http://pag.aids2012.org/abstracts.aspx?aid=21132|url=http://pag.aids2012.org/abstracts.aspx?aid=21132|url-status=usurped|publisher=AIDS 2012 conference, Washington DC|title=Evaluation of treatment with the histone deacetylase inhibitor vorinostat (suberoylanilide hydroxamic acid; SAHA) in antiretroviral drug treated, SIVmac239-infected rhesus macaques|archive-date=2016-09-19}}</ref>
* 12 March
** Researchers at the [[Vienna University of Technology]] develop a [[3D printer]] that can print at the nano-scale and is orders of magnitude faster than previous devices.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2012/03/120312101918.htm|title=3-D Printer with Nano-Precision|publisher=Science Daily|date=2012-03-12|access-date=2021-05-29}}</ref>
** A diet high in [[red meat]] can shorten [[life expectancy]] by increasing the risk of death from cancer and heart problems, according to a study of more than 120,000 people by researchers at Harvard Medical School. Substituting red meat with fish, chicken or nuts lowered the risks, the study found.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/health-17345967|title=Red meat increases death, cancer and heart risk, says study|publisher=BBC|date=2012-03-14|access-date=2021-05-29}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal|last1=Pan|first1=An|last2=Sun|first2=Qi|last3=Bernstein|first3=Adam N.|display-authors=1|title=Red Meat Consumption and Mortality: Results From 2 Prospective Cohort Studies|journal=Archives of Internal Medicine|year=2012|volume=172|issue=7|pages=555–563|doi=10.1001/archinternmed.2011.2287|pmid=22412075|pmc=3712342}}</ref>
* 13 March
** A California-based company has developed solar panels that are half the price of today's cheapest cells, and therefore cheap enough to challenge fossil fuels.<ref>{{cite news | last=Anthony | first=Sebastian | title=Solar panel made with ion cannon is cheap enough to challenge fossil fuels | website=ExtremeTech | date=2012-03-13 | url=http://www.extremetech.com/extreme/122231-solar-panels-made-with-ion-cannon-are-cheap-enough-to-challenge-fossil-fuels | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref>
** Scientists have identified a potential drug that speeds up trash removal from the cell's recycling center, the [[lysosome]], one of the causes of [[aging]] and degenerative diseases.<ref>{{cite web | last=Michigan | first=University of | title=Biologists find potential drug that speeds cellular recycling | website=Medical Xpress | date=2012-03-13 | url=https://medicalxpress.com/news/2012-03-biologists-potential-drug-cellular-recycling.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Shen | first1=Dongbiao | last2=Wang | first2=Xiang | last3=Li | first3=Xinran | display-authors=1| title=Lipid storage disorders block lysosomal trafficking by inhibiting a TRP channel and lysosomal calcium release | journal=Nature Communications | publisher=Springer Science and Business Media LLC | volume=3 | issue=1 | year=2012 | issn=2041-1723 | doi=10.1038/ncomms1735 | page=731| pmid=22415822 | pmc=3347486 | bibcode=2012NatCo...3..731S }}</ref>
[[File:FirstNeutrinoEventAnnotated.jpg|thumb|right|250px|15 March 2012: scientists send the first coherent message using [[neutrino]]s (first recorded neutrino event pictured).]]
* 14 March
** A fly species, kept in complete darkness for 57 years (1,400 generations), showed genetic alterations that occurred as a result of environmental conditions, offering clear evidence of [[evolution]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/loom/2012/03/14/fifty-seven-years-of-darkness/ |publisher=Discover Magazine|title=Fifty-seven Years of Darkness|author=Carl Zimmer|date=2012-03-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160725051354/http://blogs.discovermagazine.com/loom/2012/03/14/fifty-seven-years-of-darkness/|archive-date=2016-07-25}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Izutsu | first1=Minako | last2=Zhou | first2=Jun | last3=Sugiyama | first3=Yuzo | display-authors=1| title=Genome Features of "Dark-Fly", a Drosophila Line Reared Long-Term in a Dark Environment | journal=PLOS ONE | publisher=Public Library of Science (PLoS) | volume=7 | issue=3 | date=2012-03-14 | issn=1932-6203 | doi=10.1371/journal.pone.0033288 | article-number=e33288| pmid=22432011 | pmc=3303825 | bibcode=2012PLoSO...733288I |doi-access=free}}</ref>
** A pill which doubles the length of time that patients with advanced [[skin cancer]] can survive has gone on sale in Britain for the first time.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/health/healthnews/9140667/Breakthrough-advanced-skin-cancer-pill-available-in-Britain.html|work=The Telegraph|title=Breakthrough advanced skin cancer pill available in Britain for the first time|author= Stephen Adams|date=2012-03-14|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120315081056/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/health/healthnews/9140667/Breakthrough-advanced-skin-cancer-pill-available-in-Britain.html|archive-date=2012-03-15}}</ref>
** America's coastlines are even more vulnerable to sea level rise than previously thought, according to a pair of new studies. Up to 32% more real estate could be affected by a 1-meter rise in sea level, while the population exposed to rising water is 87% higher than previously estimated.<ref>{{cite web | title=Sea level studies: US coasts even more vulnerable than previously thought | website=The Christian Science Monitor | date=2012-03-14 |author=	Pete Spotts| url=https://www.csmonitor.com/Environment/2012/0314/Sea-level-studies-US-coasts-even-more-vulnerable-than-previously-thought | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Strauss | first1=Benjamin H | last2=Ziemlinski | first2=Remik | last3=Weiss | first3=Jeremy L | display-authors=1 | title=Tidally adjusted estimates of topographic vulnerability to sea level rise and flooding for the contiguous United States | journal=Environmental Research Letters | publisher=IOP Publishing | volume=7 | issue=1 | date=2012-03-01 | issn=1748-9326 | doi=10.1088/1748-9326/7/1/014033 | article-number=014033| bibcode=2012ERL.....7a4033S | s2cid=53359382 | doi-access=free }}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Pederson | first1=N | last2=Bell | first2=A R | last3=Knight | first3=T A | display-authors=1| title=A long-term perspective on a modern drought in the American Southeast | journal=Environmental Research Letters | publisher=IOP Publishing | volume=7 | issue=1 | date=2012-03-01 | issn=1748-9326 | doi=10.1088/1748-9326/7/1/014034 | article-number=014034| bibcode=2012ERL.....7a4034P | s2cid=59455451 | doi-access=free }}</ref>
** A process to "unprint" toner ink from paper has been developed by engineers at the University of Cambridge, using short laser pulses to erase words and images.<ref>{{cite web | title=Laser 'unprinter' wipes photocopied ink from paper | publisher=BBC News | date=2012-03-15 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/technology-17381600 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Leal-Ayala | first1=David Ricardo | last2=Allwood | first2=J. M. | last3=Schmidt | first3=M. | display-authors=1| title=Toner-print removal from paper by long and ultrashort pulsed lasers | journal=Proceedings of the Royal Society A: Mathematical, Physical and Engineering Sciences | publisher=The Royal Society | volume=468 | issue=2144 | date=2012-03-14 | issn=1364-5021 | doi=10.1098/rspa.2011.0601 | pages=2272–2293| bibcode=2012RSPSA.468.2272L | doi-access=free }}</ref>
* 15 March – American scientists use a particle accelerator to send a coherent [[neutrino]] message through 780 feet of rock. This marks the first use of neutrinos for communication, and future research may permit binary neutrino messages to be sent immense distances through even the densest materials, such as the Earth's core.<ref>{{cite web | last=Boyle | first=Rebecca | title=For the First Time, a Message Sent With Neutrinos | website=Popular Science | date=2019-03-18 | url=https://www.popsci.com/science/article/2012-03/first-time-neutrinos-send-message-through-bedrock/ | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=STANCIL | first1=D. D. | last2=ADAMSON | first2=P. | last3=ALANIA | first3=M. | display-authors=1| title=Demonstration of Communication Using Neutrinos | journal=Modern Physics Letters A | volume=27 | issue=12 | date=2012-04-13 | pages=1250077-1-1250077-10 | issn=0217-7323 | doi=10.1142/s0217732312500770 | arxiv=1203.2847 | bibcode=2012MPLA...2750077S | s2cid=119237711 }}</ref>
* 16 March – Physicists found no discernible difference between the speed of a neutrino and the [[speed of light]] in latest test of the [[faster-than-light neutrino anomaly]].<ref>{{cite web | title=A Challenge to Einstein' Theory Falls in Retest of Neutrinos' Speed | website=The New York Times | date=2012-03-17 | url=https://www.nytimes.com/2012/03/17/science/einstein-challenge-falls-in-retest-of-neutrinos-speed.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | last=Palmer | first=Jason | title=Neutrinos clocked at light-speed in new Icarus test | publisher=BBC News | date=2012-03-16 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-17364682 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Antonello | first1=M. | last2=Aprili | first2=P. | last3=Baiboussinov | first3=B. | display-authors=1| title=Measurement of the neutrino velocity with the ICARUS detector at the CNGS beam | journal=Physics Letters B | publisher=Elsevier BV | volume=713 | issue=1 | year=2012 | issn=0370-2693 | doi=10.1016/j.physletb.2012.05.033 | pages=17–22|arxiv=1203.3433| bibcode=2012PhLB..713...17A | s2cid=55397067 }}</ref>
* 18 March
** Researchers have identified why a mutation in a particular gene can lead to [[obesity]].<ref>{{cite web | title=Obesity gene's role revealed in mice study | publisher=BBC News | date=2012-03-18 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/health-17398746 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Liao | first1=Guey-Ying | last2=An | first2=Juan Ji | last3=Gharami | first3=Kusumika | display-authors=1| title=Dendritically targeted Bdnf mRNA is essential for energy balance and response to leptin | journal=Nature Medicine | publisher=Springer Science and Business Media LLC | volume=18 | issue=4 | date=2012-03-18 | issn=1078-8956 | doi=10.1038/nm.2687 | pages=564–571| pmid=22426422 | pmc=3327556 }}</ref>
** [[NEC]] has developed "organic radical battery" (ORB) technology with a thickness of just 0.3mm.<ref>{{cite web | last=Owano | first=Nancy | title=NEC goes ultra-thin with 0.3mm-thick batteries | website=Phys.org | date=2012-03-18 | url=https://phys.org/news/2012-03-nec-ultra-thin-03mm-thick-batteries.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref>
* 19 March
** Even if humankind manages to limit [[global warming]] to {{convert|2|°C|°F|abbr=on}}, as the [[Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change]] recommends, future generations will have to deal with [[sea levels]] {{convert|12|to|22|m|ft|sp=us}} higher than at present, according to research published in the journal ''Geology''.<ref>{{cite web|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120321211534/http://news.rutgers.edu/medrel/news-releases/2012/03/global-sea-level-lik-20120316|publisher=Rutgers|title=Global Sea Level Likely to Rise as Much as 70 Feet for Future Generations|author=Ken Miller|date=2012-03-19|archive-date=2012-03-21|url=http://news.rutgers.edu/medrel/news-releases/2012/03/global-sea-level-lik-20120316}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Miller | first1=K. G. | last2=Wright | first2=J. D. | last3=Browning | first3=J. V. | display-authors=1| title=High tide of the warm Pliocene: Implications of global sea level for Antarctic deglaciation | journal=Geology | publisher=Geological Society of America | volume=40 | issue=5 | date=2012-03-19 | issn=0091-7613 | doi=10.1130/g32869.1 | pages=407–410| bibcode=2012Geo....40..407M}}</ref>
** Researchers at the RIKEN Advanced Science Institute (Japan) have developed a way to create full-color [[holograms]] with the aid of surface plasmons.<ref>{{cite web | last=Kawata | first=Satoshi | title=New technique lights up the creation of holograms | website=Phys.org | date=2012-03-19 | url=https://phys.org/news/2012-03-technique-creation-holograms.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref>
** The amount of photovoltaic [[solar panels]] installed in the US more than doubled from 2010 to 2011, according to a report by the Solar Energy Industries Association (SEIA) and GTM Research.<ref>{{cite web | last=Hull | first=Dana | title=Solar installations doubled last year, with California leading the way | website=Phys.org | date=2012-03-19 | url=https://phys.org/news/2012-03-solar-year-california.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref>
** [[Seagate Technology|Seagate]] claims it has paved the way for 3.5-inch hard drives with 60&nbsp;TB capacities, after breaking the 1&nbsp;TB/square inch density threshold.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.pcpro.co.uk/news/373651/seagate-creates-1tb-square-inch-hard-drives|publisher=PC Pro|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120329193304/http://www.pcpro.co.uk/news/373651/seagate-creates-1tb-square-inch-hard-drives|archive-date=2012-03-29|date=2012-03-19|title=Seagate creates 1TB/square inch hard drives|author=Stewart Mitchell}}</ref>
[[File:Solar panels in Ogiinuur.jpg|thumb|right|250px|19 March 2012: researchers report that the number of [[solar panels]] in the United States more than doubled between 2010 and 2011.]]
* 20 March
** Astronomers have discovered the first known rectangular-shaped [[galaxy]]: [[LEDA 074886]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.technologyreview.com/blog/arxiv/27657/?p1=blogs|publisher=Technology Review|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120324184517/http://www.technologyreview.com/blog/arxiv/27657/?p1=blogs|archive-date=2012-03-24|title=Astronomers Discover Rectangular Galaxy|date=2012-03-20}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Graham | first1=Alister W. | last2=Spitler | first2=Lee R. | last3=Forbes | first3=Duncan A. | display-authors=1| title=Leda 074886: A Remarkable Rectangular-Looking Galaxy | journal=The Astrophysical Journal | publisher=American Astronomical Society | volume=750 | issue=2 | date=2012-04-24 | issn=0004-637X | doi=10.1088/0004-637x/750/2/121 | page=121| s2cid=28425342 | url=https://www.zora.uzh.ch/id/eprint/70060/1/1203.3608v1.pdf | arxiv=1203.3608 | bibcode=2012ApJ...750..121G }}</ref>
** New analysis by MIT shows that there is enough room underground to safely store at least a century's worth of U.S. [[Carbon emissions|fossil fuel emissions]].<ref>{{cite web | last=Chandler | first=David L. | title=Greenhouse gas can find a home underground | website=MIT News &#124; Massachusetts Institute of Technology | date=2012-03-19 | url=https://news.mit.edu/2012/greenhouse-gas-in-aquifers-0320 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Szulczewski | first1=M. L. | last2=MacMinn | first2=C. W. | last3=Herzog | first3=H. J. | display-authors=1| title=Lifetime of carbon capture and storage as a climate-change mitigation technology | journal=Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences | volume=109 | issue=14 | date=2012-03-19 | issn=0027-8424 | doi=10.1073/pnas.1115347109 | pages=5185–5189| pmid=22431639 | pmc=3325663 | doi-access=free }}</ref>
* 24 March – Humans hunted Australia's giant vertebrates to extinction about [[Upper Paleolithic#40 000 BC|40,000 years ago]], the latest research published in ''Science'' has concluded.<ref>{{cite web | title='Humans killed off Australia's giant beasts' | publisher=BBC News | date=2012-03-24 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-17488447 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Rule | first1=S. | last2=Brook | first2=B. W. | last3=Haberle | first3=S. G. | display-authors=1| title=The Aftermath of Megafaunal Extinction: Ecosystem Transformation in Pleistocene Australia | journal=Science | publisher=American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) | volume=335 | issue=6075 | date=2012-03-22 | issn=0036-8075 | doi=10.1126/science.1214261 | pages=1483–1486| pmid=22442481 | bibcode=2012Sci...335.1483R | s2cid=26675232 }}</ref>
* 25 March
** Global temperatures could rise by {{convert|3.0|°C|°F|abbr=on}} by 2050, a new computer simulation has suggested.<ref>{{cite web| title=Temperatures could rise by 3C by 2050, models suggest | publisher=BBC News | date=2012-03-25 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-17488450 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Rowlands | first1=Daniel J. | last2=Frame | first2=David J. | last3=Ackerley | first3=Duncan | display-authors=1| title=Broad range of 2050 warming from an observationally constrained large climate model ensemble | journal=Nature Geoscience | publisher=Springer Science and Business Media LLC | volume=5 | issue=4 | date=2012-03-25 | issn=1752-0894 | doi=10.1038/ngeo1430 | pages=256–260| bibcode=2012NatGe...5..256R | hdl=10871/9287 | hdl-access=free}}</ref>
** Canadian film director [[James Cameron]] reaches the [[Challenger Deep]], the deepest known point in Earth's oceans, in the ''[[Deepsea Challenger]]'' submersible. Cameron is the first person to visit the Deep, which is located in the Pacific [[Mariana Trench]], since 1960.<ref>{{cite web | title=James Cameron Completes Record-Breaking Mariana Trench Dive | website=Adventure | date=2012-03-25 | url=https://www.nationalgeographic.com/adventure/article/120325-james-cameron-mariana-trench-challenger-deepest-returns-science-sub | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210220224755/https://www.nationalgeographic.com/adventure/article/120325-james-cameron-mariana-trench-challenger-deepest-returns-science-sub | archive-date=20 February 2021 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=James Cameron's Submarine Trip to Challenger Deep | website=The New York Times | date=2012-03-25 | url=https://www.nytimes.com/2012/03/26/science/james-camerons-submarine-trip-to-challenger-deep.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title=James Cameron completes journey to deepest spot on Earth | publisher=NBC News | date=2012-03-25 | url=https://www.nbcnews.com/id/wbna46850002 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref>
** Physicists report that the largest molecules yet tested (molecules containing 58 or 114 atoms) also demonstrate [[wave–particle duality|quantum wave behavior]] using the classic [[double-slit experiment]].<ref>{{cite web | last=Moskowitz | first=Clara | title=Largest Molecules Yet Behave Like Waves in Quantum Double-Slit Experiment | website=livescience.com | date=2012-03-25 | url=https://www.livescience.com/19268-quantum-double-slit-experiment-largest-molecules.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Juffmann | first1=Thomas | last2=Milic | first2=Adriana | last3=Müllneritsch | first3=Michael | display-authors=1| title=Real-time single-molecule imaging of quantum interference | journal=Nature Nanotechnology | volume=7 | issue=5 | date=2012-03-25 | issn=1748-3387 | doi=10.1038/nnano.2012.34 | pages=297–300| pmid=22447163 | arxiv=1402.1867 | bibcode=2012NatNa...7..297J | s2cid=5918772 }}</ref>
* 28 March – [[NASA]] announces the name of the [[Mars|Martian]] mountain, [[Mount Sharp (Mars)|Mount Sharp]], that the [[Mars Science Laboratory]] rover (also known as "Curiosity") will explore after its planned landing in [[Gale Crater]] on 6 August 2012.<ref>{{cite web | title=NASA's New Mars Rover Will Explore Towering 'Mount Sharp' | website=Space.com | date=2012-03-29 | url=https://www.space.com/15097-mars-mountain-sharp-curiosity-rover.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web | title='Mount Sharp' On Mars Links Geology's Past and Future | website=NASA | date=2012-03-28 | url=http://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/msl/news/msl20120328.html | access-date=2021-06-06 | archive-date=3 March 2016 | archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160303205729/http://www.nasa.gov/mission_pages/msl/news/msl20120328.html }}</ref>
* 29 March
** "Solar tornadoes" several times as wide as the Earth have been observed in the Sun's atmosphere by the Atmospheric Imaging Assembly telescope on board NASA's [[Solar Dynamics Observatory]] (SDO) satellite.<ref>{{cite web | last=Society | first=Royal Astronomical | title=Huge tornadoes discovered on the Sun | website=Phys.org | date=2012-03-29 | url=https://phys.org/news/2012-03-huge-tornadoes-sun.html | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref>
** Scientists have revealed the most detailed picture of the [[Milky Way galaxy]] ever produced, with over a billion stars visible in a mosaic combined from thousands of individual images.<ref>{{cite web | last=Amos | first=Jonathan | title=Picture captures a billion stars | publisher=BBC News | date=2012-03-29 | url=https://www.bbc.com/news/science-environment-17539315 | access-date=2021-06-06}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=http://djer.roe.ac.uk/vsa/vvv/iipmooviewer-2.0-beta/vvvgps5.html |publisher=The Royal Observatory, Edinburgh|access-date=2021-06-06|title=MooViewer 2.0}}</ref>
** New scanning technology has revealed that the human brain possesses an astonishingly simple 3D grid structure, with sheets of parallel neuronal fibers crossing one another at right angles.<ref>{{cite web | title=Brain wiring a no-brainer? Scans reveal astonishingly simple 3D grid structure | website=Medical Xpress | date=2012-03-29 | url=https://medicalxpress.com/news/2012-03-brain-wiring-no-brainer-scans-reveal.html | access-date=2021-06-07}}</ref><ref>{{cite journal | last1=Wedeen | first1=V. J. | last2=Rosene | first2=D. L. | last3=Wang | first3=R. | display-authors=1| title=The Geometric Structure of the Brain Fiber Pathways | journal=Science | publisher=American Association for the Advancement of Science (AAAS) | volume=335 | issue=6076 | date=2012-03-29 | issn=0036-8075 | doi=10.1126/science.1215280 | pages=1628–1634| pmid=22461612 | pmc=3773464 | bibcode=2012Sci...335.1628W }}</ref>

==Deaths==
